commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=476e8dff0db3f009daa954972aaba4c9ee41ab26)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a transient right-click Reply workflow to Dashboard webchat with a reply preview and Markdown quote prepended on send.

PR surface: Source +219. Total +219 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as an existing-behavior bug, but the PR defects are source-reproducible: the PR head's app-render caller omits the new reply props, and CI reports the added Escape handler lint failure.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor or maintainer revision plus exact-branch browser proof; missing contributor proof keeps it out of the automated repair lane.

target.title: feat(control-ui): add right-click Reply in Dashboard webchat
target.body:
## Summary - Adds right-click Reply to messages in the Dashboard / Control UI webchat - Right-click any chat bubble → "Reply" option → reply preview bar appears above the composer → type reply → message auto-prepended with Markdown blockquote - Click ✕ on the preview bar or press Escape to cancel the reply - No storage/migration changes — uses composer state only, prepends a clean `> **Sender:** quoted text` block on send ### Implementation notes 1. **Context menu showed `[object Object]Reply`** — `icons.messageSquare` (a lit-html `TemplateResult`) was interpolated into `.innerHTML` string, which stringified to `[object Object]`. Fixed by using a raw SVG string with `fill="currentColor"` in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts:1668`. 2. **Reply preview dismiss ✕ button invisible** — The `.chat-reply-preview__dismiss` CSS class had no `svg` child rule, so the `icons.x` SVG (bare `` elements with default `fill: black; stroke: none`) rendered invisibly. Fixed by adding `.chat-reply-preview__dismiss svg { stroke: currentColor; fill: none; stroke-width: 2px; }` in `ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css`. 3. **Reply quote was silently dropped on send** — `handleSendChat` called `enqueuePendingSendMessage` with the **original** `message` BEFORE computing `effectiveMessage = prependReplyQuote(...)`. Then `sendChatMessageNow` was called with `effectiveMessage` + `queueItemId`, but the function ignores the `message` param when `queueItemId` is set — it reads the queued item's original text. Fixed by moving `prependReplyQuote` BEFORE `enqueuePendingSendMessage`. 4. **Double `>` in single-line quotes** — `prependReplyQuote` produced `> **User:** > hello world` for single-line messages because it unconditionally prepended `> ` to each line then joined with the `> **${label}:**` prefix. Fixed by branching: single-line uses `> **${label}:** ${text}`, multi-line uses `> **${label}:**\n> line1\n> line2`. ### Quote format examples (what the model receives) | Quoted message | Your reply | Sent to model | |---|---|---| | `hello world` | `got it` | `> **User:** hello world`

`got it` | | `line one`
`line two` | `ok` | `> **User:**`
`> line one`
`> line two`

`ok` | ## Linked context Closes #16896 ## Real behavior proof - **Behavior addressed:** Users previously had to manually copy-paste messages to reference them. Now right-click → Reply sets a reply target with a visual preview, and the message is automatically quoted on send. - **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw dev build (vite), WSL2 Ubuntu 22.04 on Windows 11, Chromium - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. `cd ui && pnpm dev` to start Control UI dev server 2. Right-click any chat message bubble 3. Verify context menu shows speech-bubble icon + "Reply" (not "[object Object]Reply") 4. Select "Reply" from the context menu 5. Verify the reply preview bar appears above the composer with a visible ✕ dismiss button 6. Type a response and send 7. Verify the sent message **actually includes** a clean `> **User:** ...` Markdown blockquote (no double `>`) 8. Verify ✕ dismiss button clears the reply target - **Evidence after fix:** - **Observed result after fix:** Right-click reply works end-to-end. Context menu shows proper icon. Preview bar appears with sender name, quoted text, and visible dismiss button. Sending prepends a clean Markdown blockquote. Dismissing clears the state. Plain send without reply target is unaffected. - **Before evidence:** Before this change, the Control UI had no reply affordance at all — users had to manually copy text and paste with `>` prefix. - **What was not tested:** Long message truncation in preview (>120 chars), keyboard shortcut (`r` key), interaction with slash commands while reply target is active, queueing behavior when chat is busy - **Proof limitations:** Tested via dev server only; production build not verified. The feature uses standard DOM APIs (contextmenu event, textContent extraction) and Lit template rendering — no new dependencies or framework-specific hacks. ## Tests and validation - `git diff --stat` shows 3 files changed: `app-chat.ts`, `views/chat.ts`, `layout.css` - Manually verified: right-click → Reply menu shows proper icon and text - Manually verified: reply preview bar ✕ button is visible and functional - Manually verified: quoted message format is clean Markdown (single-line and multi-line) - Manually verified: ✕ dismiss clears reply state - Manually verified: plain send (no reply) unaffected - No automated test added: this is a UI interaction feature; the existing chat view test suite covers rendering paths ## Risk checklist - **User-visible behavior change:** Yes — new right-click Reply feature in Control UI - **Config/environment/migration change:** No - **Security/auth/secrets/network/tool execution change:** No - **Highest-risk area:** DOM text extraction from chat bubbles may include whitespace noise; mitigated by `.trim().slice(0, 500)` - **Risk mitigation:** Reply target is stored in transient state only (not persisted). The prepend logic is a pure string function with no side effects. ## Current review state - **Next action:** Ready for maintainer review - **Waiting on:** Nothing
target.author: programmingwtf
